description: Approved Data Analytics golden reference templates for generated Dashboard pages and Data List forms. Generated analytics controls must select one template by business need, clone the approved export-shaped template, and preserve its style, layout, typography, and surface contract.

references.lockedRule: The pie_chart_with_title_wrapper subtree and every non-editable descendant must preserve the exported style, layout, and typography settings. Only data-source binding inside pie_chart_container and the business text value of pie_chart_title may change.
whenToUse: Use when users need part-to-whole distribution by category, status, type, owner group, or priority.
Use when the business question is which category makes up the largest share of a known total.
Use when the category count is small enough for a readable legend, typically two to six meaningful slices.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for time-series trends or ordered date movement.
Do not use when many categories make the pie chart unreadable.
Do not use when precise ranking or side-by-side comparison is more important than share of total.
references.summary: Card-style pie chart module with a custom title and a compact legend for part-to-whole composition.
references.requiredAppPlanDeclaration: Select data_analytics_pie_chart_with_title for a Dashboard/Data List form analytics region when the business question is distribution or share of a total. Declare the source object, category/grouping field, aggregate/count field, title text, and why the category set is small enough for a pie chart.
generationProof: pie_chart_with_title_wrapper root subtree
pie_chart_title Text control mapped to a business title
pie_chart_container data binding mapped to a legal source and category/aggregate fields
template provenance on the wrapper or analytics control
references.proofBoundary: Export-shaped pie chart template only; not proof for time-series, ranking, pivot, funnel, gauge, or custom chart layouts.

references.lockedRule: The column_chart_with_title_wrapper subtree and every non-editable descendant must preserve the exported style, layout, and typography settings. Only data-source binding inside column_chart_container and the business text value of column_chart_title may change.
whenToUse: Use when users need vertical comparison across statuses, priorities, categories, teams, owners, or periods.
Use when column height should be the primary signal for count, total, or numeric aggregate comparison.
Use when labels are short enough to fit under vertical columns.
whenNotToUse: Do not use when horizontal label space is required; use the bar chart template instead.
Do not use for part-to-whole composition where share of total is the main question.
Do not use for cross-tab row/column matrix analysis; use pivot table instead.
references.summary: Card-style vertical column chart module with a custom title for comparing values across categories or periods.
references.requiredAppPlanDeclaration: Select data_analytics_column_chart_with_title when the analytics region needs vertical category or period comparison. Declare the source object, x-axis/grouping field, y-axis aggregate, title text, and why vertical columns fit the labels.
generationProof: column_chart_with_title_wrapper root subtree
column_chart_title Text control mapped to a business title
column_chart_container data binding mapped to legal category and aggregate fields
template provenance on the wrapper or analytics control
references.proofBoundary: Export-shaped column chart template only; not proof for horizontal bar ranking, area trends, line trends, pivot matrices, or custom chart layouts.

references.lockedRule: The bar_chart_with_title_wrapper subtree and every non-editable descendant must preserve the exported style, layout, and typography settings. Only data-source binding inside bar_chart_container and the business text value of bar_chart_title may change.
whenToUse: Use when users need horizontal comparison across categories with longer labels.
Use for ranked counts, request types, issue categories, backlog distribution, queue load, or owner/team workload comparisons.
Use when the y-axis category labels need more room than a column chart provides.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for time-series movement where a date or ordered period is the primary x-axis.
Do not use when share of total is the main question; use pie chart if category count is small.
Do not use when row/column cross-tab totals are needed; use pivot table instead.
references.summary: Card-style horizontal bar chart module with a custom title for ranked or category comparison with longer labels.
references.requiredAppPlanDeclaration: Select data_analytics_bar_chart_with_title when the analytics region needs horizontal category/ranking comparison. Declare the source object, y-axis category field, aggregate/count field, title text, and why horizontal labels are preferable.
generationProof: bar_chart_with_title_wrapper root subtree
bar_chart_title Text control mapped to a business title
bar_chart_container data binding mapped to legal category and aggregate fields
template provenance on the wrapper or analytics control
references.proofBoundary: Export-shaped bar chart template only; not proof for vertical columns, line/area trends, pivot matrices, or custom chart layouts.

references.lockedRule: The line_chart_with_title_wrapper subtree and every non-editable descendant must preserve the exported style, layout, and typography settings. Only data-source binding inside line_chart_container and the business text value of line_chart_title may change.
whenToUse: Use when users need trend movement over time or another ordered dimension.
Use for created, completed, approved, overdue, usage, volume, SLA, or throughput trends by date or period.
Use when the shape, peaks, dips, and direction of the trend are more important than filled volume.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for categorical part-to-whole distribution.
Do not use when there is no time, date, period, or ordered dimension.
Do not use when cumulative magnitude or filled area emphasis is the primary visual need; use area chart instead.
references.summary: Card-style line chart module with a custom title for trend movement over ordered time or sequence fields.
references.requiredAppPlanDeclaration: Select data_analytics_line_chart_with_title when the analytics region needs a line trend over date/period/order. Declare the source object, date/period field, aggregate/count field, title text, and why line movement is the best signal.
generationProof: line_chart_with_title_wrapper root subtree
line_chart_title Text control mapped to a business title
line_chart_container data binding mapped to legal date/period and aggregate fields
template provenance on the wrapper or analytics control
references.proofBoundary: Export-shaped line chart template only; not proof for area-filled trends, categorical ranking, pie distribution, pivot matrices, or custom chart layouts.

references.lockedRule: The area_chart_with_title_wrapper subtree and every non-editable descendant must preserve the exported style, layout, and typography settings. Only data-source binding inside area_chart_container and the business text value of area_chart_title may change.
whenToUse: Use when users need trend movement over time with filled area emphasis.
Use for cumulative volume, utilization, demand, capacity, workload, or backlog trends where magnitude over time matters.
Use when the business audience should quickly see both direction and total visual weight of the trend.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for categorical part-to-whole distribution.
Do not use when there is no time, date, period, or ordered dimension.
Do not use when exact point-to-point trend shape is more important than magnitude emphasis; use line chart instead.
references.summary: Card-style area chart module with a custom title for time or ordered trends where the filled area emphasizes volume or magnitude.
references.requiredAppPlanDeclaration: Select data_analytics_area_chart_with_title when the analytics region needs a filled trend over date/period/order. Declare the source object, date/period field, aggregate/count field, title text, and why filled area magnitude is useful.
generationProof: area_chart_with_title_wrapper root subtree
area_chart_title Text control mapped to a business title
area_chart_container data binding mapped to legal date/period and aggregate fields
template provenance on the wrapper or analytics control
references.proofBoundary: Export-shaped area chart template implemented with the approved line-chart control shape; not proof for generic line charts, stacked area variants, pie distribution, pivot matrices, or custom chart layouts.

references.lockedRule: The pivot_table_standard control must preserve all exported style settings. Only legal data-source, rows, columns, and values binding may be mapped to the current app.
whenToUse: Use when users need cross-tab analysis by row, column, and aggregate value.
Use for operational matrices such as category by team, status by owner, period by type, priority by department, or impact by support group.
Use when users need to compare multiple intersections instead of a single chart axis.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for chart-only visual comparison.
Do not use when users need a simple single-axis category chart.
Do not use on Approval forms.
references.summary: Standard pivot table analytics control for cross-tab analysis by row, column, and aggregate values.
references.requiredAppPlanDeclaration: Select data_analytics_pivot_table_standard when the analytics region needs row/column/value cross-tab analysis. Declare the source object, row fields, column fields, value aggregate fields, and why matrix analysis is required.
generationProof: pivot_table_standard control identity
legal source binding
configured rows, columns, and values from real fields
template provenance on the pivot control
references.proofBoundary: Export-shaped pivot table template only; not proof for charts, editable grids, Collection tables, or Approval form analytics.
